text:
There's no remote configured — this repo has no `origin` set up. To push, you'll need to add a remote. Run:

```bash
git remote add origin https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/YOUR_REPO.git
git push -u origin HEAD
```

Or if you're using SSH:
```bash
git remote add origin git@github.com:YOUR_USERNAME/YOUR_REPO.git
git push -u origin HEAD
```

Note: the repo is in a detached HEAD state, so `HEAD` will push the current commits. If you want to push to a named branch, use `git push -u origin HEAD:main` (or whatever branch name you want).
